methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Theion, a Series A-stage frontier-tech company in Climate & Energy founded by Ulrich Ehmes, has raised €25M+ to date. The valuation exercise draws on a sparse comparable universe: 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers operating across the sector, including later-stage companies such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable here. The Climate & Energy sector contains only 9 same-sector comparables, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. A sector-wide median would conflate companies at materially different development and funding stages, introducing substantial noise rather than signal.

The thinness of the peer set reflects the structural reality of frontier-tech climate ventures: few players reach scale, and stage clustering is sparse. Without sufficient same-stage or near-stage comparables, conventional multiples-based valuation anchoring is not defensible. Any post-money valuation estimate for Theion would rest primarily on alternative methods (DCF assumptions, venture capital methodology, or strategic positioning relative to the later-stage cohort) rather than market comparables. The key uncertainty is whether Theion's capital efficiency and technical trajectory align with the capital intensity and timelines of the peer set, most of which operate at higher funding stages.
